# frozen_string_literal: true

module Api
  module Public
    module V3
      # v3 api for creating plaid link tokens
      class PlaidLinkTokensController < BaseController
        skip_before_action :set_account
        skip_before_action :set_action_page

        def create
          client_user_id = current_user.present? ? current_user.uid : SecureRandom.hex(10)
          token = PlaidApi.new.link_token(client_user_id)

          render json: { token: }, status: :ok
        end

        def identity
          token = PlaidApi.new.identity_link_token(current_user, identity_params)

          render json: { token: }, status: :ok
        end

        def identity_params
          params.require(params_wrapper).permit(
            :address_city,
            :address_country,
            :address_line1,
            :address_line2,
            :address_postal_code,
            :address_region,
            :date_of_birth,
            :first_name,
            :last_name,
            :ssn
          )
        end

        WRAPPED_MODEL_PARAMS = %w[
          addressCity
          addressCountry
          addressLine1
          addressLine2
          addressPostalCode
          addressRegion
          dateOfBirth
          firstName
          lastName
          ssn
        ].freeze

        wrap_parameters params_wrapper, include: WRAPPED_MODEL_PARAMS
      end
    end
  end
end

# frozen_string_literal: true

module Api
  module Public
    module V3
      # base for public controllers
      class BaseController < ApiApplicationController
        before_action :set_account
        # before_action :set_yodlee_required_amount
        before_action :set_action_page

        def set_account
          @account = if params[:custom_domain]
                       CustomDomain.activated.find_by!(
                         hostname: params[:custom_domain]
                       ).account
                     elsif params[:account_slug]
                       Account.active.lookup_by_slug(params[:account_slug])
                     else
                       return head :not_found
                     end
        end

        def set_action_page
          @action_page = @account.action_pages.active
                                 .with_slug_or_id(params[:action_page_slug]).first!
        end

        def cache_response
          request.session_options[:skip] = true
          expires_in 30.seconds, public: true
        end
      end
    end
  end
end

# frozen_string_literal: true

# method for wrapping incoming json params
module ParamsWrapper
  extend ActiveSupport::Concern

  # creates wrapper from controller class, exposing on class/instance
  module Helper
    def params_wrapper
      scope = is_a?(Class) ? self : self.class
      scope.name.split("::").last.remove("Controller").underscore
    end
  end

  included { include Helper }
  class_methods { include Helper }
end

# frozen_string_literal: true

require "swagger_helper"

RSpec.describe "Plaid Link Tokens", openapi_spec: PUBLIC_V3_DOC do
  path "/public/v3/plaid_link_tokens" do
    post "Create a plaid link token" do
      tags "Plaid Link Tokens"
      consumes "application/json"
      produces "application/json"

      context "when success" do
        let(:link_token) { "token" }

        before do
          allow_any_instance_of(PlaidApi).to receive(:link_token).and_return(link_token)
        end

        response "200", :created do
          run_strict_test! do |response|
            res = JSON.parse(response.body)

            expect(res["token"]).to eq(link_token)
          end
        end
      end
    end
  end

  path "/public/v3/plaid_link_tokens/identity" do
    post "Create a plaid identity link token" do
      tags "Plaid Link Tokens"
      consumes "application/json"
      produces "application/json"
      parameter name: :params, in: :body, schema: {
        type: :object,
        properties: {
          addressCity: { type: :string },
          addressCountry: { type: :string },
          addressLine1: { type: :string },
          addressLine2: { type: :string },
          addressPostalCode: { type: :string },
          addressRegion: { type: :string },
          dateOfBirth: { type: :string },
          firstName: { type: :string },
          lastName: { type: :string },
          ssn: { type: :string }
        }
      }

      context "when success" do
        let(:user) { create(:user) }
        let(:token) { "token" }
        let(:params) do
          {
            addressCity: "New York",
            addressCountry: "US",
            addressLine1: "123 Main St",
            addressLine2: "Apt 4B",
            addressPostalCode: "10001",
            addressRegion: "NY",
            dateOfBirth: "1990-01-01",
            firstName: "John",
            lastName: "Doe",
            ssn: "123456789"
          }
        end

        before do
          login_as(user)
          allow_any_instance_of(PlaidApi).to receive(:identity_link_token).and_return(token)
        end

        response "200", :created do
          run_strict_test! do |response|
            res = JSON.parse(response.body)

            expect(res["token"]).to eq(token)
          end
        end
      end
    end
  end
end

# frozen_string_literal: true

# base class for v3 api
class ApiApplicationController < ApplicationController
  rescue_from StandardError,
              ArgumentError,
              NotImplementedError, with: :internal_server_error
  rescue_from ActionController::ParameterMissing, with: :unprocessable_content
  rescue_from Pundit::NotAuthorizedError, with: :forbidden
  rescue_from ActiveRecord::RecordNotFound, with: :not_found

  include Pagination
  include ParamsWrapper
  include Idempotency

  before_action :clean_pagination_params
  before_action :transform_params_keys
  before_action :reject_html_requests

  protect_from_forgery

private

  def serialize_collection(collection, status: :ok, **opts)
    if opts.delete(:without_count)
      collection = collection.without_count
      opts[:meta] ||= {
        previousPage: collection.prev_page,
        currentPage: collection.current_page,
        nextPage: collection.next_page
      }
    else
      opts[:meta] ||= {
        currentPage: collection.current_page,
        totalPages: collection.total_pages,
        totalCount: collection.total_count
      }
    end

    opts[:root] = :data

    serialize(collection, status, opts)
  end

  def serialize_obj(obj, status: :ok, **opts)
    serialize(obj, status, opts)
  end

  def serialize(data, status, opts)
    serializer = serializer_class(opts[:serializer] || self.class)

    render json: serializer.render(data, opts),
           status:
  end

  def serialize_errors_for(obj, status: :unprocessable_content)
    render json: ErrorSerializer.render(obj), status:
  end

  def clean_pagination_params
    request.params.delete_if { |k, v| %w[page per_page].include?(k) && v.blank? }
  end

  def transform_params_keys
    request.params.deep_transform_keys! { |k| k.to_s.underscore }
  end

  def reject_html_requests
    head :not_acceptable if request.format.html?
  end

  def serializer_class(klass)
    return klass if klass.to_s.include?("Serializer")

    Object.const_get(klass.to_s.remove("Controller").singularize << "Serializer")
  end

  def internal_server_error(err)
    Anedot::ExceptionLogger.error(err)
    head :internal_server_error
  end

  def unprocessable_content(err)
    Anedot::ExceptionLogger.warn(err)
    head :unprocessable_content
  end

  def forbidden
    head :forbidden
  end

  def not_found

**Explaining parameter requirement and error handling**

I'll highlight that the `identity_params` method requires a `plaid_link_token` key due to strong parameter enforcement, which causes a `ParameterMissing` error when absent, referencing relevant controller lines. I’ll explain that this requirement means the request must send a JSON body (not a query string) for `wrap_parameters` to create the needed nested params, and that the `ApiApplicationController` rescues this error by returning a 422 status. This clarifies why the user sees the 422 and helps finalize the plan with specific line references.

payload.text:
**Summarizing root cause and fix**

I’m preparing a final message that highlights how `before_action :transform_params_keys` ensures underscores, while `wrap_parameters` only processes JSON bodies—not query parameters. I’ll reference the `ParameterMissing` 422 error in `plaid_link_tokens_controller.rb` due to missing nested params and mention the rescue to 422 in `api_application_controller.rb`. I plan to include instructions to fix the issue by sending a POST request with a JSON body, with examples and relevant code line references. Next, I’ll wrap this up with a clear bullet-point summary and suggest verifying by hitting the endpoint with JSON.

summary.text:
**Summarizing root cause and fix**

I’m preparing a final message that highlights how `before_action :transform_params_keys` ensures underscores, while `wrap_parameters` only processes JSON bodies—not query parameters. I’ll reference the `ParameterMissing` 422 error in `plaid_link_tokens_controller.rb` due to missing nested params and mention the rescue to 422 in `api_application_controller.rb`. I plan to include instructions to fix the issue by sending a POST request with a JSON body, with examples and relevant code line references. Next, I’ll wrap this up with a clear bullet-point summary and suggest verifying by hitting the endpoint with JSON.

payload.message:
**Findings**
- The identity endpoint pulls its params from `params.require(params_wrapper)` (`params_wrapper` → `plaid_link_token`), so the controller only permits values nested under that key (`app/controllers/api/public/v3/plaid_link_tokens_controller.rb:24-36`). When the request is made with query-string params (e.g., `?address_city=…`), Rails never builds `params[:plaid_link_token]`, so `ActionController::ParameterMissing` is raised and the API returns 422 via the rescue in `app/controllers/api_application_controller.rb:5-9,83-86`.
- `wrap_parameters params_wrapper, include: …` (`app/controllers/api/public/v3/plaid_link_tokens_controller.rb:39-52`) only fires for JSON bodies. Posting JSON such as `{ "addressCity": "Golden", ... }` (or the snake_case equivalents) with `Content-Type: application/json` will automatically wrap your attributes under `plaid_link_token`, satisfying the strong params check.

**Next Steps**
1. Ensure you’re issuing a POST and place the values in the JSON body rather than the query string, e.g.:

```http
POST /public/v3/plaid_link_tokens/identity
Content-Type: application/json

{
  "addressCity": "Golden",
  "addressCountry": "US",
  "addressLine1": "855 Visionary Trail",
  "addressPostalCode": "80401",
  "addressRegion": "CO",
  "dateOfBirth": "1988-04-04",
  "firstName": "System",
  "lastName": "Admin",
  "ssn": "456456456"
}
```

2. Retry the call; if you still see issues, check that the user is authenticated (since the controller reads `current_user`).

content.text:
**Findings**
- The identity endpoint pulls its params from `params.require(params_wrapper)` (`params_wrapper` → `plaid_link_token`), so the controller only permits values nested under that key (`app/controllers/api/public/v3/plaid_link_tokens_controller.rb:24-36`). When the request is made with query-string params (e.g., `?address_city=…`), Rails never builds `params[:plaid_link_token]`, so `ActionController::ParameterMissing` is raised and the API returns 422 via the rescue in `app/controllers/api_application_controller.rb:5-9,83-86`.
- `wrap_parameters params_wrapper, include: …` (`app/controllers/api/public/v3/plaid_link_tokens_controller.rb:39-52`) only fires for JSON bodies. Posting JSON such as `{ "addressCity": "Golden", ... }` (or the snake_case equivalents) with `Content-Type: application/json` will automatically wrap your attributes under `plaid_link_token`, satisfying the strong params check.

**Next Steps**
1. Ensure you’re issuing a POST and place the values in the JSON body rather than the query string, e.g.:

```http
POST /public/v3/plaid_link_tokens/identity
Content-Type: application/json

{
  "addressCity": "Golden",
  "addressCountry": "US",
  "addressLine1": "855 Visionary Trail",
  "addressPostalCode": "80401",
  "addressRegion": "CO",
  "dateOfBirth": "1988-04-04",
  "firstName": "System",
  "lastName": "Admin",
  "ssn": "456456456"
}
```

2. Retry the call; if you still see issues, check that the user is authenticated (since the controller reads `current_user`).
in the code, what indicates that the params should be in the body?

payload.message: in the code, what indicates that the params should be in the body?

- `identity_params` demands `params.require(params_wrapper)` before permitting (`app/controllers/api/public/v3/plaid_link_tokens_controller.rb:24-36`). Because `params_wrapper` resolves to `plaid_link_token`, the controller looks for a top-level JSON object named `plaid_link_token` (or one created via wrapping) before it even inspects the fields. Query-string params never populate that nested key, so they can’t pass the `require`.
- `wrap_parameters params_wrapper, include: WRAPPED_MODEL_PARAMS` (`app/controllers/api/public/v3/plaid_link_tokens_controller.rb:39-52`) only runs for JSON request bodies. It wraps the listed attributes under `plaid_link_token`, satisfying the `require` call above. Without a JSON body—i.e., when the params arrive in the query string—there’s nothing to wrap, so the controller raises `ActionController::ParameterMissing` and you get the 422.

content.text:
- `identity_params` demands `params.require(params_wrapper)` before permitting (`app/controllers/api/public/v3/plaid_link_tokens_controller.rb:24-36`). Because `params_wrapper` resolves to `plaid_link_token`, the controller looks for a top-level JSON object named `plaid_link_token` (or one created via wrapping) before it even inspects the fields. Query-string params never populate that nested key, so they can’t pass the `require`.
- `wrap_parameters params_wrapper, include: WRAPPED_MODEL_PARAMS` (`app/controllers/api/public/v3/plaid_link_tokens_controller.rb:39-52`) only runs for JSON request bodies. It wraps the listed attributes under `plaid_link_token`, satisfying the `require` call above. Without a JSON body—i.e., when the params arrive in the query string—there’s nothing to wrap, so the controller raises `ActionController::ParameterMissing` and you get the 422.

payload.text:
**Analyzing parameter wrapping issue**

I’m figuring out why the wrapper is missing when snake_case JSON is sent but wrap_parameters expects camelCase fields. It looks like wrap_parameters checks keys before any underscore transformation, so sending snake_case fields without a root or camelCase prevents proper wrapping. The user must send JSON with camelCase keys matching the include list or nest snake_case keys under the expected root for wrapping to work correctly.

payload.message:
- The controller calls `wrap_parameters params_wrapper, include: WRAPPED_MODEL_PARAMS` and that include list only contains camelCase names (`addressCity`, `dateOfBirth`, …) (`app/controllers/api/public/v3/plaid_link_tokens_controller.rb:39-52`). Rails will only auto-wrap keys that exactly match that list.  
- Because your body uses snake_case (`address_city`, etc.), none of those keys are wrapped under `plaid_link_token`, so `params.require(params_wrapper)` still raises `ActionController::ParameterMissing` (`app/controllers/api/public/v3/plaid_link_tokens_controller.rb:24-36`).  

Two fixes:  
1. Send camelCase field names (e.g., `addressCity`) with `Content-Type: application/json`, or  
2. Manually nest the body: `{ "plaid_link_token": { "address_city": "Golden", ... } }`.  

Either way, the controller will then see `params[:plaid_link_token]` and the 422 should go away.

summary.text:
**Analyzing parameter wrapping issue**

I’m figuring out why the wrapper is missing when snake_case JSON is sent but wrap_parameters expects camelCase fields. It looks like wrap_parameters checks keys before any underscore transformation, so sending snake_case fields without a root or camelCase prevents proper wrapping. The user must send JSON with camelCase keys matching the include list or nest snake_case keys under the expected root for wrapping to work correctly.

content.text:
- The controller calls `wrap_parameters params_wrapper, include: WRAPPED_MODEL_PARAMS` and that include list only contains camelCase names (`addressCity`, `dateOfBirth`, …) (`app/controllers/api/public/v3/plaid_link_tokens_controller.rb:39-52`). Rails will only auto-wrap keys that exactly match that list.  
- Because your body uses snake_case (`address_city`, etc.), none of those keys are wrapped under `plaid_link_token`, so `params.require(params_wrapper)` still raises `ActionController::ParameterMissing` (`app/controllers/api/public/v3/plaid_link_tokens_controller.rb:24-36`).  

Two fixes:  
1. Send camelCase field names (e.g., `addressCity`) with `Content-Type: application/json`, or  
2. Manually nest the body: `{ "plaid_link_token": { "address_city": "Golden", ... } }`.  

Either way, the controller will then see `params[:plaid_link_token]` and the 422 should go away.
